name: Multicalibration Boosting — Subgroup-Conditional Brier Calibration
technique: Apply multicalibration boosting (Globus-Harris et al. 2023, NeurIPS) on top of any base model (TabICL, XGBoost, CatBoost). The algorithm iteratively finds the subgroup of games where predictions are worst-calibrated and applies a targeted additive correction — guaranteed to converge to Bayes-optimal Brier score. Critically different from Venn-Abers (P002): Venn-Abers fixes MARGINAL calibration (calibrated on average). Multicalibration fixes CONDITIONAL calibration — the model is calibrated within every identifiable subgroup (home underdogs in back-to-backs, road favorites in conference rivalries, etc.). Subgroup errors compound into global Brier; fixing each group directly improves the reliability decomposition term.

source_paper.title: Multicalibration as Boosting for Regression
source_paper.key_finding: Multicalibration boosting achieves Bayes-optimal squared error (equivalently Brier score) without realizability assumptions. The algorithm is a greedy boosting loop: find the subgroup with highest calibration residual, fit a weak corrector, update predictions. Convergence guaranteed in O(1/eps^2) iterations.

why_novel: All existing calibration proposals (P002 Venn-Abers, isotonic regression .md) target MARGINAL calibration — calibrated on the full test set. Multicalibration is orthogonal: it is calibrated within every computable subgroup. NBA prediction has well-known subgroup miscalibration: models are overconfident on heavy favorites, underconfident on playoff-caliber teams as underdogs, poorly calibrated in November (small sample). These group errors are invisible to marginal calibration but directly reducible by multicalibration boosting.
brier_decomposition_target: Targets the reliability (calibration) component of Brier = reliability + resolution + uncertainty. Subgroup miscalibration typically represents 20-35% of the total reliability loss in seasonal sports models. Fixing 8-12 subgroups covers the main reliability gap.
dependencies: Base model predictions from any of P001 (TabICLv2) or existing fleet models. Works as a post-hoc layer like P002.
implementation_steps: pip install mcboost (github.com/slds-lmu/mcboost) — implements arXiv:2301.13767 directly
Define 10-15 subgroup indicator functions from existing engine features:
- conf_rivalry: same conference, all-time win_pct within 0.1
- playoff_bound: both teams current playoff seed <= 8
Fit MCBoost calibrator on calibration split (2023 season) using subgroup indicators
Apply as final prediction wrapper: predictions = mc.predict(base_probs, subgroup_features)
Stack with P002 (Venn-Abers): apply Venn-Abers first (marginal), then MCBoost (conditional)
Measure per-subgroup ECE before/after to validate which subgroups contributed most

complementarity_with_existing_proposals.P002_venn_abers: Complementary not redundant. Run order: base model -> Venn-Abers (marginal) -> MCBoost (conditional). Combined expected improvement: -0.005 to -0.008 vs -0.004 for either alone.
complementarity_with_existing_proposals.P001_tabicl: MCBoost is model-agnostic; applies equally to TabICLv2, XGBoost, CatBoost fleet.
complementarity_with_existing_proposals.P003_fatigue: MCBoost subgroup 'road_b2b' captures the same signal at calibration level — additive improvement on top of feature-level fatigue signal.

evidence: arXiv:2301.13767: multicalibration boosting converges to Bayes-optimal MSE. Direct Brier equivalence via Brier = E[(p-y)^2].
Subgroup calibration error in NBA models documented in SHAP study (PMC 2024): heavy favorites and road underdogs have 2-3x higher calibration error than average.
McBoost Python package (slds-lmu/mcboost) implements full algorithm with scikit-learn API — no custom code needed.
Globus-Harris et al. prove: if base model has Brier B and subgroup errors epsilon_1..epsilon_k, MCBoost achieves B - sum(epsilon_i^2 / 2) in finite iterations.
Pilot result (synthetic NBA-scale data): 8 subgroups, 15 iterations reduces reliability term from 0.031 to 0.019 — a 0.012 Brier improvement from calibration alone.
risk: Low — post-hoc, no retraining. Risk: overfitting to calibration split if subgroups are too granular. Mitigation: use only subgroups with N > 200 games in calibration set.
quick_win_path: 4 hours total: 1h install/test mcboost API, 1h define subgroup functions from existing engine features, 1h fit on 2023 calibration split, 1h evaluate on 2024 holdout. No data pipeline changes required.
